rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
When AGREE reports type errors like '<error>' or 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace the root cause to invalid identifiers or helper declarations and correct or remove them.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions and helpers have consistent and resolvable types to avoid validation errors.
Only reference visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex scope to prevent unresolved reference diagnostics.
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for const and eq declarations to prevent resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference valid expressions with known types; avoid using '<error>' types from unresolved references.
Assign statements in implementation scopes must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce property references or identifiers that are not declared in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assume/guarantee in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
